reels.transcript: 33 in two days, one thing I've realized as I've caught an older is that I've become really good at working. Not necessarily living working, if there's a problem I'll solve it, if there's a hole I'll chase it, if there's something to build I'll build it and honestly I'm proud of that but somewhere along the way I forgot how to do things just because they make me happy. When I was a kid I was endlessly curious, I wanted to try everything, every spot, every hobby, every random activity that caught my attention. I didn't care if I was good at it, I just wanted to experience it. Somewhere would be growing up and getting busy that curiosity got pushed to the side. This year I started learning how to swim, which is funny because I wanted to do it for ever. I'm literally a water baby but every year there was always a reason not to. To busy, too much work after this project, after this launch, after this trip. And one day I realized I've been saying later to myself for years. Not just with swimming, with so many things, the hobbies I wanted to try, the books I wanted to read, the classes I thought would be fun, small adventures I kept putting off, the mini version of me that just wanted to do it. And I think that's what growing up has taught me life isn't only the big milestone, it's also random to use the afternoons, the hobby of that at, trying something new, just because it sounds interesting. Coffee with a friend laughing so hard your stomach hurts. The little things don't happen in between life, they are life. So this year I'm trying something different, less I'll do it later, more, why not now.
reels.caption:
33 in 2 days ✨

Turns out adulting is remembering that hobbies exist.

Not everything needs to become a side hustle.
Not everything needs to be useful.
Not everything needs to make money.

Also, because it’s birthday week and I want to celebrate with you guys too, I’m doing a little giveaway. 💛

What’s one thing you’ve always wanted to learn, try, or do… but keep telling yourself “one day”?

I’ll be randomly picking 2 winners on 29th June and announcing them on my Instagram Stories.

Can’t wait to read your answers. 🥹

reels.transcript: 33 tomorrow, both this always make me emotional. I don't know if anyone else feels this way, but every year around my birthday, I start subtracting on everything. The things that went right, things that didn't, the world enough, me, I was at the beginning of the year and the version of me sitting here today. A few days ago, I found myself feeling really over, not because something was wrong, just because life felt full. Work, family, dreams, plans, responsibilities, and for a moment, it felt like my brain had 47 dabs open at the same time. I think both this do that, they make you look back, they make you look ahead and sometimes they can make you realize just how much life can change in a world. But after sitting with it for a bit, I realized something. A lot of things I used to dream about are already part of my life, the people I love, the work I care about, the freedom to build things that excite me, the chance to keep learning new things about myself and know. I don't have everything figured out, I still over thing, I still get over and I still start way too many projects, but I'm also learning how to swim, learning how to rest, learning how to enjoy things before immediately moving on to the next world, this one's really important action. And maybe most importantly, I'm building a better relationship with myself, more a perfect one, because I don't think so that's even possible, but much kind of one, we are growing together, we are figuring things out together, I don't know why both this, but this emotional it's basically new, it was even just for me. But honestly, you couldn't pay me enough to go back to my 20s or this year, about your 30s, I think they might be right, to know yourself better, you care less about the wrong thing. And for the first time in a long time, I'm actually excited about what's ahead. So here's to 33, to try new things, to change in your mind, to becoming more yourself, to all the versions of us with things, and all the ones we still get to become. I think the future looks pretty good from here.
reels.caption:
33 tomorrow 🥹💛

Birthdays always make me emotional.

Maybe it’s the reflecting.
Maybe it’s the nostalgia. suddenly has me thinking about everything that’s changed in the last year. 😭

But somewhere between the chaos, the lessons, the mistakes, the wins, the tears, the laughter, and all the random plot twists…

I think I’ve become a little more myself.

And honestly, that’s a pretty nice feeling.

Also, what they say about your thirties?

I think they might be right.

You couldn’t pay me enough to go back to my twenties. 😂

Thank you for growing up with me, for cheering me on, and for being part of this little corner of the internet.

And because it’s birthday week, I’m still picking 2 giveaway winners on 29th June and announcing them on my Instagram Stories. ✨

reels.transcript: One thing for joy, everyday day 24 and I'm in Meghalaya. Today I'm going to taste something that I've been waiting to taste for four years. Basically we have these berries called kafal in Uttarakhand which I've grown up eating and I have missed every season because it's a very small season of it for like the last 10 years. But four years ago or something I stumbled upon a reel which showed the berries of Meghalaya and they are very close to kafal, in fact they are much much bigger and today I'm finally tasting them. This is our guide on our trip this time. So this is the sophie berry which is, this one is green, this is the smaller size. Then do we have a red here? Here are the reds and the greens. Hello! Thank you. And this, I don't know who this is. It's tagged along. Time to taste. I want to try the red one first because we have red kafals. This is literally like kafal but like much bigger which just makes it even better. I'm going to try the green one which is supposed to be more sour. Really good though. I love khatta. So the masala that they've put is really tasty. I think it's like a homemade namak sort of thing which we also have in our Uttarakhand. But the fruits here are lovely so if you want to come to Meghalaya just come for the fruits if nothing else but there's a lot of other things. Hi! Say hi! How are you doing?

reels.transcript: बुद्वार बेटा, तुमने सुबा से दर्वाजा नहीं खोला है। देखो, कीड़ा चीनी पास्ता और बलडी अरबियाटा सौस। खोलो! कीड़ीश! येस बेबी श्री. कीड़ीश! बुद्वार बेटा, पापा जा रहे है बर्क पे शम्शान घाट। क्या लेकियाओ मैं अपनी बाड़ी बेबी गरल के लिए? बताओ! बेटा सोनू निगम नहीं ला पाऊंगा मैं। ओके? चल मैं चलता हूँ काम पे। बुद्वार बेटा, तुम्हारे फेवरेट शोका एपिसोड आया है। आपका ख़तम हो चुका है। अरे नाक्षी बेटा, तुम यहां? नाक्षी और उसकी फैमिली सारे भोसडी गए हैं। उसका गाउ है। बुद्वार, चीज तुम्हारी परस्टनल डायरी खोद के ले आया है। देखते हैं, क्या? मैं पर परवार करता हूँ, साथ लाक अठटर बार। मैं करना नहीं चाहती थी पर जजंतरम ममन्तरम। उसने पहले ही ततंतरम फफंतरम का मंतर रगा दिया। छोड़ो चीज, तुम और मैं कुलफी खाने चलते हैं। मैंने बोला है न नॉक किया करो आप। आम बुद्वार, तुम जानती हो आदम परिवार आम नहीं खा सकता। इने खाके हमें क्या होता है, तुम्हें पता है। लाओ। नहीं। मात।

हलो? हांजी मम्मी. सलोनी, तु घर कब आईगी, मुझे ये बता दे. अरे आ जाओंगी घर, अभी तो आई हूँ मैं बाहर. तु है कहां? कहां हूँ मतलब क्या, मैंने बोला था न, मुझे काम है, इसलिए बाहर जाना है. फ़ुरण घर आ जा तु. अरे भाई, बड़ी हो गई हूँ मैं, थोड़ा तो मुझे अकेला छोड़ दो. अम्मा जी को चिंता हो रही है, पूछ रही है तु कब तक आईगी. बोल दो न अम्मा जी को कुछ भी, बहाना मारो. तु मेरा बीपी मत बढ़धा. मेरे बाहर रहने से आपका बीपी कैसे बढ़ रहा है? घर आ तु तुरंट. आ रही हूँ घर, ठीक है. सॉरी यार, I don't think हमारे बीच ये हो पाएगा. अब शादी का क्या सोचा है फिर तुने? कोई लड़का हो तो बता दे, हमें कोई दिक्कत नहीं, हम तेरी पसंद से करवा देंगे शादी. कैसा मुझ बना रही है? इतने इतने घंटे तु भार रहती है, तेरा जैसा कोई बॉइफरेंड ना हो. हमने ते इतनी आजादी दे रखी है, फिर भी पता ना इस अउलाद को और कितनी आजादी चाहिए.

reels.transcript: कां गई थी मुम्मी आज आप? अरे सलोनी आज पूछ मत आज वे खतम हो गए. कौन? अरे वे. अरे आम खतम हो गए क्या? भाई मेरे ले लाओ मुझे खाने के साथ चाहिए होते हैं. अरे बेवकूफ तेरे ताओ जी के सालू खतम हो गए आज. अच्छा, कैसे? बिमार थे? नहीं, बिमार तो बिल्कुल ना थे. सुबह पोती को स्कूल छोड़ने गए हैं. भाई अपने हिसाब से फिट थे वो. उनका वजन तो लेकिन काफी जादा था. आपको याद है वो हमारे घर आय थे और सीड़ी भी नहीं चड़पाय थे? ना ना वो तो पहले था, अब तो मेरे खाल से 90 किलो के थे. पर मुझे तो याद है वो पीते थे. एक बार हमारे फंक्शन में आके उन्होंने पीके इतना नाटक नहीं किया था? हाँ, शराब तो पीते, पर अब मैंने सुनी जैसे के एक महिने से शराब भी छोड़ी हुई. और ऐसे तो पार्क में भी 5-6 चक्कर लगाते हैं. सुबह बस एक बुरी आदती के बीडी साथ ही चलती है, उनके चक्कर लगाते वे भी साथ ही. सुबह वैसे फिट हैं, वैसे कोई बिमारी नहीं होने. और तो और बिमारी चाहिए भी क्या इसके बाद भाई? ना जैसे आजकल हाट अटैक इतना सुनने में आए कि जी वो सोते सोते ही खतम हो गया, अटैक पड़ गया. ऐसी उने कभी कुछ दिक्कत नहीं हुई, अब कोलेस्ट्रॉल था, अब वो तो हमें तुम्हें जैसे कोई हो जाये, वो खान पान ही इतना खराब है, शुगर थी उन्हें कुछ तीस साल से, पर इसके एलावा और कोई, मतलब ऐसा कोई बड़ी विमारी नहीं थी, कि भाई अब बिस्तर पे ही पड़ गये हो, दूसरों से काम कराना पड़ रहा, अपने सारे काम खुद कर लेते, इतने फिट तो थे हो. हाँ, ये सब सुनके तो लग नहीं रा हो कि उनको कुछ बिमारी थी, हट्टे कट्टे आदमी को उठा लिया भगवान ने. हाँ, ऐसे ही है आजकल तो.

reels.transcript: चल चुपचाप, ज़्यादा वाज़ नहीं करनी। भाईया मेरे चपल रह गई, भाईया! हम नंगे मेर नहीं चल सकते, भाईया! प्लीज चपल ले जाने दो! चल सोझा चुपचाप! यहाँ पर जमीन पे? भाईया, आप लोग तो बहुत बिज़ी रहते होगे, पोचा भी नहीं लग पाता होगा. तो अगर मुझे बेड मिल जाता तो... भाईया, अगर एक टावल भी मिल जाता तो गंदा पिलो मेरे फेस पर टच होता है तो मेरे पिमपल निकल आते हैं. बहुत जुमान चल रही है तेरी ले! भाईया, यह आपने दोया था न? हाँ, दोया था. अच्छा, फिर एक बार डेटॉल के पानी से भी निकाल लो प्लीज. भाईया, आपने मेरे घर पर फिराउती के लिए फोन कर दिया? पर्सो तक का टाइम दिया है. तो भाईया, उस ते पहले मेरे अंडर गामेंट्स बंगवा लो न उनसे प्लीज. सेम अंडर वियर एक दिन से सबगाँ नहीं पहनते भाईया. बहुत जुबान चल रही है तेरी. भाईया, आपने मेरा मुझ पने गंदे हाथों से टच कर दिया, अपने घर से टेन स्टीम कोरियन स्किन केर रुटीन करके आई थी. अब मेरा पक्का पिमपल निकलेगा. चल खाना खा ले. भाईया, आपने सबजे धो कर तो बनाई है न? अच्छा कोई बात नहीं, जो भी है दे दो, उबलने से तो वैसे भी सारे जर्म्स मरी गए होंगे. ये ले अपने बाप से बात कर और समझाए इसे. भाईया, आपने अभी यही फोन अपने गाल पर लगा हो था, अपने गाल पर भी लगा दिया, साफ तो करके देते. अच्छा स्पीकर पर कर दो. हेलो पापा, आप प्लीज पैसे से पहले सेनिटाइजर भेज दो, इनके मारने से पहले मैं गंदीगी से मर जाओंगी.

reels.transcript: I had a crush on this guy when I was in college. He supported, he sweet, he literally ticked everything I wanted in a guy, but he saw me as his little sister. He was seeing somebody at that time and I was like, I'm not going to cross the line. But then he graduated, I graduated, we still kept in touch though. However, his conversation would always start with, hey kid. And he goes like, don't lower your standards, don't ever settle for anything less, you're amazing, you're beautiful, the man who's with you should know that. And I was like, aww. Is this the time where I tell him that I actually like him? Maybe I could like get out of the sister zone. And he goes, I waited for five years and here's my gorgeous girlfriend that I'm madly in love with. She's the love of my life. And I want to know, stop here. And he sent me this beautiful picture of him and his girlfriend, who's gorgeous by the way. They're perfect together. It's just not easy to take it. The guys who I want them to see me as a sister and a friend do not see me that way. And the guy who I want to be madly in love with is not in love with me. I am so happy for them, but I'm also very sad for myself.

reels.transcript: My first trip to Italy was in September 2024. It was a solo birthday trip and while I was in Rome, I met Andrea who then introduced me to a few of his friends. And the last day of my trip, I ended up cooking Indian food for all of them and that is still one of my core memories. What do we name this as? Chapati Hapati. It's been almost two years now and we've all stayed in touch. So when Cannes happened, I knew I wanted a small break after everything. I had booked my concert in Milan on the 22nd and Rome is genuinely one of my favorite cities. So I thought, why not go back and see these people again? I texted them and they invited me over for potluck. At first, I thought I'd cook something nice with whatever groceries I could find. I brought mushrooms, pasta and completely ruined it. I don't even have the footage because I was crying. I was genuinely upset though because I was so excited about it. But also, maybe Italians don't need to eat my Indianized version of a pasta anyway. So I changed the plans. Now I had a brinjal and some mushrooms. So I made brinjal fry and butter garlic pepper mushrooms instead. It was not bad actually. Then I got dressed in this pretty fit, packed everything and left. And honestly, the second I reached there, it just felt really nice. I hugged everybody. Got kurdas for Andrea and Filippo, earrings for the girls and watching everyone get excited over these tiny gifts made me so happy. Look there! We were all prepping for the potluck, went up to the terrace, set the table and it was one of those moments where everything feels very soft and quiet for a second. We saw the sunset, everybody just got together, sharing stories, ate food. Reina is trying Indian food. What do you think about it, Reina? Do you like it? It's the best thing in the world. We laughed our hearts out, started playing music. And I think the whole evening just felt very special to me because what do you mean I have friends in another country now? This will definitely be another core memory I have now. Love you, bye!
reels.caption:
Thank you so much for having me, Andrea, Filippo, Mai (@andrea.brilli @riccifilippo @maibrittanava ) and all the new faces I met and became friends with.
With so many tourist attractions in Rome, I think the coolest thing I get to share is that I went to a potluck with my friends there.

It’s crazy to see how much you guys have grown in the last two years. I missed my girl @princesse_camcam .
Thank you for everything. This will definitely be another core memory for me.
Only sending love and light your way. 🤍

reels.transcript: When the moon hits your eye like a big pizza pie, that's amore. When the world seems to shine like you've had too much wine, that's amore.
reels.caption:
🍝 Episode 3: The Time I Ate the Best Pasta in Rome

Rome 2026 was SO delicious.

This was only my second pasta in Italy.
So I kept thinking:
“Surely the next pasta will be even better.”

It wasn’t. 😂😭

🍝What I ordered:
-Amatriciana 
-and a plate of Roman artichokes.

Ive eaten an Amatriciana before but ohhhh boy!!!

This one was was…
rich, peppery, deeply savoury… tasted both bold and comforting at the same time. 

I was heading to a whole day of exploring the Vatican…and I devoured this meal in less than 20 mins, and gosh it delivered and HOW!

When I thought of Rome, I expected- the people, the life, and overall vibe to stun me, but this Amatriciana was something out of this world.

Was I too hungry to rate it a 10/10 or was it actually the best pasta Ive had in my life?!! we will never know.

you go! and you tell me!!!

reels.caption:
An itchy plant can turn into a wearable t-shirt? 

For generations, the Lepcha community living in Dzongu Valley, Sikkim, has transformed stinging nettle into fabric using skills passed down through families. 

Where many of us see an ordinary forest plant, indigenous communities like these see resources, respect and a way of living in harmony with nature.

It’s a beautiful reminder that here, nature isn’t just worshipped, it is utilised in some of the most sustainable and creative ways we can think of.

This is a centuries-old tradition that’s still practised in parts of Sikkim and Uttarakhand. Supporting crafts like these helps preserve livelihoods, revive indigenous traditions and keep this incredible knowledge alive.

So, would you wear clothing made from stinging nettle?

📍Nettle Handloom training & Production Centre, Hee-gyathang village, Dzongu
📍The main person/organisation behind these efforts is
MLAS NGO DZONGU and Ecostrem Gangtok
📍And if someone wants to purchase nettle woven fabric/tshirt contact 96791 78961, 9474522599 or mlasngo@gmail.com

Divided by state lines, language, ethnicity and religion, but aren’t we all united by the feeling of loving and being loved by our fathers? 

So how do you say ‘Papa’? 

This video is a representation of some of the ways in which we call our mothers in India. Happy Father’s Day! 

“How India says Papa”:

- నాన్న (Nanna)(Andhra PradeshTelangana)

- அப்பா (Appa)(Tamil Nadu)

- ತಂದೆ (Tande)(Karnataka) 

- അച്ഛൻ (Achan)(Kerala)

- बाबा (Baba) (Maharashtra)

- બાપુ/બાપુજી (Bapu/Bapuji)(Gujarat & Rajasthan)

- বাবা (Baba) (West Bengal)

- ਬਾਊ ਜੀ/ਪਿਓ (Bauji/Pyo)(Punjab)

reels.transcript: जहां इंटरनेट की दुनिया की ये North India वरसेस South India वाली लड़ाई कभी खतम नहीं होती, वहीं मेरी फैमिली में कुछ अलग ही हो रहा है. हाई, मैं तान्या और मैं आयी थी केला मेरी काजन की शादी में टू अमलियाली बॉय. और ये हमारी पंजाबी शादियों से बहुत डिफरेंट थी. ये एक मंदिर में हुई और मुझे पहली बार यहां के कस्टम्स का पता चला. लाइक देस एक ड्रेस कोड फो में, शर्ट्स और बन्यान उतार के ही एंट्री मिलती है. बैकराउंड में क्लासिकल कारनार्टिक मीजिक बजरा था. और मेरी कजन बहुत ज़्यादा सुन्दर लग रही थी. उसने ट्रिदिशनल कासुव साडी पहनी और बालों में गजरा. कस्टम्स बिल्कुल ही सिम्पिल और सुइट थे और वोचिंग देम में में बहुत अपना पर खुश रहा था. और मेरी परिवाल में ये पहली क्रॉस कल्चरल शादी नहीं है. एक पर मेरे कजिन्स जस्ट प्रपोस टू ए तेलगू गरल, अपने परिवाल पर खुश रहा था और मैं पर खुश रहा था एक कच्छी गुजराती पर. मैं बहुत परिवाल पर खुश रहा हूँ जो परिवाल पर खुश रहा था और लव पर खुश रहा था. और कुछ भी इंपोज या कलेश करने की बजाए, सब ने अपने तरीके से इस शादी का हिस्सा बनने की कोशिश की. जैसे मैंने अपने पापा को पहली बार मुन में देखा और अगर मैं पर अगर ये इंडिया के लिए पर परवारत रहा हूँ, दिखने काल्शर्ज हमें डिवाइड नहीं, बल्कि हमारी खुश्यों को मल्टिप्लाइ करते हैं. और एक फैमिली के लिए अलग काल्शर में जुड़ने का मतलब है कि हमें नहीं परवारत रहा हूँ, तो अब मैंने भी सोच लिया. मुझे तो इस शादी के दोरान अपनी नई मल्याली फैमिली से दोस्ती करनी है, तो तो नेक्स्ट ओनम, मुझे एक खुश्यों के लिए अगर नहीं परवारत रहा हूँ, तो आपकी फैमिली में कोई टू स्टेट्स वाली शादी होई है?

reels.transcript: If you're currently doing wedding shopping or planning on buying jewelry, then you need to know this. I especially went to attend the DBS Natural Diamond Masterclass, where they showed us the most exquisite selection of rough and natural diamonds. Yellow diamonds are formed when nitrogen gets incorporated into the diamond crystals and it forms over 100 miles below the surface. Green diamonds get their colors from natural irradiation when the diamond surface is exposed to radioactive minerals over millions of years. Violets are one of the rarest and are associated with hydrogen or nickel-nitrogen related defects, while orange diamonds are thought to be linked to oxygen-related defects that give them the warm glow. Even the transparent diamonds you see in their raw form are not the same. Some look cloudy like this and some are clear like this. And interestingly, all natural diamonds begin in an octahedral crystal shape before transforming into different forms over time. It is crazy to think how these diamonds are 1 to 3.5 billion years old and they were bought closer to the surface by volcanic eruptions. You know diamonds need in between 900 degree Celsius and 1300 degree Celsius to form. Anything outside of this, the carbon turns into graphite, which for your information is used in your pencil. The history, the coincidences that require to happen to make this natural diamond is truly what makes it special. I am lucky to have family heirlooms passed down from my great grandmother's wedding 80 to 100 years back and I want to continue the tradition and make heirlooms that I can pass down to the next generation.
reels.caption:
DID YOU KNOW THIS ABOUT DIAMONDS?!

I recently attended a De Beers Natural Diamond Masterclass, where they showcased some of the most exquisite rough and natural diamonds.

And honestly, I was blown away by how much there is to know about diamonds.

From rare yellow, green, violet and orange diamonds to understanding how they are formed over millions of years, every detail was fascinating.

What surprised me the most was that even the transparent diamonds we see in jewellery look completely different in their natural, rough form.

The history behind them, and the incredible series of events that need to happen for a natural diamond to form, is what makes them so special.

I’m lucky to have family heirlooms that were passed down from my great-grandmother’s wedding nearly 80 years ago. It makes me appreciate the idea of creating heirlooms that can be passed on to future generations as well.

As someone who is getting married soon, learning all of this through De Beers has been incredibly useful.

[Anushka Rathod, Finance, India, Diamonds, De Beers]
#anushkarathod #finance #india #diamonds #debeers

reels.transcript: होम लोन वालों सिर्फ ये एक हैक से आप लाखों बचा सकते हो, बट अगर आपने इसको सही से नहीं किया तो आपको लाखों का नुकसान भी हो सकता है. लेट मी एक्स्प्लेइंट. तो होम लोन का ही एक वेरियंट होता है होम लोन ओडी. अलग-अलग बैंक में इसे अलग-अलग नाम से बुलाया जाता है लाइक दीस. पर ये होम लोन ओडी है क्या और क्या इसे आपको ओपट करना चाहिए? देखो होम लोन ओडी में आपके सेविंग्स अकाउंट को आपकी होम लोन अकाउंट से लिंक कर दिया जाता है. इसका सबसे बड़ा फाइदा यह है कि आपकी जो भी सेविंग्स है उतने से आपका होम लोन अमांट कम हो जाएगा. तो उतने अमांट पे आपका 7-8% का इंट्रेस्ट बचेगा. फ़ोर इक्साम्पल अगर एक करोड का आपका होम लोन है और सामने आपके 10 लाग रुपे रखे वे आपकी सेविंग्स अकाउंट में तो बैंक आपको सिफ 90 लाग पे इंट्रेस्ट चार्च करेगी ना कि पूरे 1 करोड पे. और फ्यूचर में जबी भी आपको अपने पैसे की जरुत हो आप अपने सेविंग्स अकाउंट से विड्ड्रॉह कर सकते हो अन आटमाटिकली आपका जो होम लोन अमांट है वो उतने अमांट से बढ़ जाएगा. Compared to अगर आपने वही पैसे को यफडी में डाले होते तो आपका वहाँ पे post tax से 4-5% return बनता. तो क्या हर home loan वाले को ये लेना चाहिए? Well नहीं. कुछ banks इसे पे actually premium charge करते हैं of up to 1% तो it's not worth it. पर कुछ banks इसे पे कोई extra charge नहीं लेते या फिर बहुत minimum premium charge करते हैं. In fact अगर आपका credit score अच्छा है तो आप bank से negotiate भी कर सकते हो to remove the premium. तो ये situation में home loan OD ले लेना चाहिए. More details in caption.
reels.caption:
Save lakhs on your home loan using this hack.

But only if you use it correctly.

Check out the full reel to understand how.

A few things to keep in mind when using a Home Loan OD (Overdraft) facility:

1. Read the terms and conditions carefully. Prepayment rules, interest rates, and any premium charges can vary from bank to bank. The core concept remains the same, but the way it works may differ slightly.

2. The money you keep in the linked savings account should ideally be for short-term goals or emergencies. Don’t park money there just for the sake of reducing interest. Have a clear purpose for that money.

3. Do a quick cost-benefit analysis. For example, if the bank charges an additional premium and you’re only keeping a small amount in the account, the interest savings may not be significant, even negative.

4. If you have a good credit score and repayment history, try negotiating with the bank. There have been cases where borrowers have successfully negotiated better terms.

Another useful option:
If you already have a home loan and need immediate funds you can consider a top-up home loan.

A top-up loan is an additional loan offered on your existing home loan. It usually comes with similar interest rates, requires less paperwork, and can be more affordable than many other borrowing options. The only catch is that approval and disbursal may take 1-2 weeks depending on the loan amount.

reels.transcript: सालरीड वालों मेरी आपसे एक request है कि 15 जून अब आ चुका है इसलिए अपने income tax portal पे जाके ये एक काम जरूर कर लेना वन्ना बाद में आपको government परिशान करेगी let me explain तो हर साल government आपका AIS यानि annual information statement prepare करती है ये समझो आपकी finance की जनम कुंडली है इसमें सारे details आपका interest, dividend, mutual fund sales, property dealing, salary सब कुछ mention होता है और ये जो सारा data है वो banks, institutions, employers को 31st May तक report करना होता है और आपके AIS में ये June 15 तक update हो जाता है for the last financial year अब अगर अपना ITR आप सही से भरते हो एकदम सारी details सही होती है बट फिर भी आपके ITR और AIS में अगर mismatch है तो government आपको notice भेज सकती है इसको avoid करना बहुत simple है आपको सिफ अपनी details verify करनी है मैं आपको step by step process बताती हूँ सबसे पहले आपको अपने documents खटे करने है employer से form 16 लेना है और बाकी documents जैसे की bank statements, mutual fund statements अगर आपका TDS, TCS pay से deduct हुआ है तो उसका receipt ये सब collect करना है उसके बाद आपको income tax portal पे अपना AIS डाउनलोड करना है and lastly आपको सारी चीज़ें जो है वो verify करनी है अब अगर आपके AIS में कोई error है जैसे की duplicate transaction या missing transaction जैसे की आपका TDS cut हुआ पर आपके AIS में दिखने ही रहा तो आपको income tax के portal पे तुरण feedback submit करना चाहिए फिर portal पे ही आपको और details पूछेंगे आपको supporting documents देने होगे and अगर claim genuine है तो आपका AIS फिर update हो जाएगा और इससे future में फिर आपको कोई problem नहीं आईगी तो अपना AIS time से check कर लेना more details in caption
reels.caption:
If you’re a salaried employee, don’t rush to file your ITR the moment the portal opens.

Check out the reel to know more 👇

Most employers, banks, mutual funds and other institutions report your FY 2025-26 data around May and June. That’s why your AIS and tax records may not be fully updated immediately and if you file using incomplete information and your AIS gets updated later, it can create mismatches that may lead to refund delays or the need to revise your return.

So, before filing, collect your Form 16, bank statements and investment statements. Then download your AIS and match everything once.

Also, TDS and TCS aren’t deducted only from your salary. They can be deducted in many other places too—for example, 10% TDS on FD interest above ₹50,000 (for non-senior citizens), 2% TCS on overseas tour packages, or 1% TCS on cars worth over ₹10 lakh.

So, make sure you collect those documents as well.

For the last FY, you can check Form 26AS for a consolidated view of TDS, TCS, and advance tax. The same information is also available in AIS. From this FY onwards, Form 26AS and AIS have been consolidated into Form 168.

[Anushka Rathod, Finance, India, Tax, AIS]
#anushkarathod #finance #india #tax #ais

reels.transcript: गोल्ड और सिल्वर फिर से इतना क्यूं गिर रहे हैं? पिछले एक हफ़ते में गोल्ड 7.5% से और सिल्वर 10% से गिरा है. समझाती हूँ क्या चल रहा है इन क्रोनलोजिकल ओर्डर? सबसे पहले 5th जून को US गवर्मेंट ने अनॉंस किया कि उनका जॉब मार्केट बढ़िया चल रहा है. उनोंने सिफ एक ही महिने में 170,000 जॉब्स को आड़ किये. अब लोगों को तो लगा था कि AI और वार के इंफलेशन के वज़े से जॉब लॉस्स होंगे जिस वज़े से एकानॉमी को रिवाइब करने के लिए जो सेंट्रल बैंक है वो इंट्रेस रेट को कम करेंगे. पर या तो उल्टा ही हो गया अब क्योंकि जॉब्स बढ़ रहे है तो सेंट्रल बैंक एंट्रेस रेट को कम नहीं करेंगे और US गवर्मेंट का जो बॉंड यील्ड है वो 4.5% तक बढ़ गया है. अब अगर US गवर्मेंट के बॉंड पे लोगों को अल्मोस्ट रिस्क फ्री 4.5% इंट्रेस मिल रहा है तो लोग गोल्ड में पैसा क्यूं डालेंगे जो नॉन यील्ड जेनेरेटिंग आसिट है? इसी रीजन के वज़े से उस दिन गोल्ड के प्राइसे 3% और सिल्वर के प्राइसे 6% से गिरे हैं. दूसरा 9th जून को इसरेल और रहान के बीच वापस से मिसाईल स्ट्राइक्स वी, जिसके वज़े से गोल्ड वापस 95 डॉलर पर बैरल पे पोज़ गया है. अब क्यूंकि ओयल का मेझर ट्रेट डॉलर में होता है तो डॉलर और स्ट्रॉंग हो गया, जिससे भी गोल्ड और सिल्वर पे नेगटिव प्रेशर आता है. और लास्टली कल ही 10th जून को यूएस गवर्मेंट ने अनॉंस किया कि उनका इंफलेशन बढ़ गया है तो 4.2%, हाईएस्ट इन थी येर्स. तो ब्रेट कट तो छोड़ो, expect क्या जा रहा है कि इंफलेशन को कंट्रोल में लाने के लिए यूएस गवर्मेंट इंट्रेस रेट को बढ़ाएंगे. तो जब यूएस गवर्मेंट के डेट पे जादा इंट्रेस मिल रहा है, तब भी बाक ही ऐसेट से पैसे निकल के यूएस गवर्मेंट के डेट पर जाएंगे और इसी ले भी गोल्ड और सिल्वर रीसेंटली करेक्ट हुआ.

reels.transcript: घर खरीते वक्त सिफी एक चीज का ध्यान रखना जिससे आपके लाखों बच सकते हैं और अगर आप इससे साइस से नहीं करो तो लाखों का नुकसान भी हो सकता है, let me explain. तो home loan का ही एक variant होता है home loan OD, अलग-अलग बांग में इससे अलग-अलग नाम से बुलाय जाता है like these. पर यह home loan OD है क्या और क्या इससे आपको opt करना चाहिए? देखो home loan OD में आपके savings account को आपके home loan account से link कर दिया जाता है. इसका सबसे बड़ा फाइदा यह है कि आपकी जो भी savings है उतने से आपका home loan amount कम हो जाएगा, तो उतने amount पे आपका 7-8% का interest बचेगा. For example, अगर एक करोड का आपका home loan है और सामने आपके 10 लाख रुपे रखे वाए आपके savings account में, तो bank आपको सिफ 90 लाख पे interest charge करेगी ना कि पूरे 1 करोड पे. और future में जबीबी आपको अपने पैसे की जरृत हो, आप अपने savings account से वो withdraw कर सकते हो और automatically आपका जो home loan amount है वो उतने amount से बढ़ जाएगा. Compare to अगर आपने वही पैसे को EFD में डाले होते, तो आपका वहाँ पे post tax से 4-5% return बनता. तो क्या हर home loan वाले को यह लेना चाहिए? Well, नहीं. कुछ banks इसपे actually premium charge करते है of up to 1%, so it's not worth it. पर कुछ banks इसपे कोई extra charge नहीं लेते या फिर बहुत minimum premium charge करते हैं. In fact, अगर आपका credit score अच्छा है, तो आप bank से negotiate भी कर सकते हो to remove the premium. तो ये situation में home loan OD ले लेना चाहिए, बस एक और चीज़ का ध्यान रखना, आपके पास ऐसा कोई short term goal होना चाहिए, जिसके वज़े से आप अपने bank account में इतने पैसे खटा करके रख रहे हो. अगर आपको short term में पैसे की जरूत ही नहीं है, तो normal home loan लो और जैसे जैसे savings होती है, वैसे वैसे home loan को pre-pay करो, don't complicate stuff. More details in caption.

reels.transcript:
इंडिया हमारा स्ट्रॉंग है, पर इतना स्ट्रॉंग भी नहीं है जितना हमें इस साल टेस्ट किया जा रहा है। इंडियन इकॉनॉमी के तीन पिलर्स हैं, रूरल डिमान, मॉनसून और ओयल। और इन तीनों में अगर एक-एक करके प्रॉबलम आती है तो हम संभाल लेते हैं। पर इस साल तीनों में एक साथ प्रॉबलम आएगी। अगनी परिक्षा ली जारी है इंडियन इकॉनॉमी के इस साल, लेट मी एक्स्प्लेइन। अब ओयल की प्रॉबलम तो आप सब जानते हो पर इस साल एक क्लाइमेटिक फेनॉमिना होने वाला है कॉल्ड एल नीनो। इन सिम्पल वर्ड्स, एल नीनो में सेंट्रिल और इस्टन पसिफिक ओशन का सर्फिस टेमप्रेचर बढ़ने लगता है अब नॉर्मल जिसे विंड साइकल रिवर्स हो जाते हैं। अब कंट्री वाईस देखें तो इससे पेरू, एक्वेडार और पार्ट्स अफ सदन यूएस में हेवी रेंफॉल और फलडिंग होती हैं। और इंडिया, उस्ट्रेलिया, इंडुनेशिया और पार्ट्स अफ आफ्रिका में ड्राउथ जैसे सिचुएशन होते हैं। अब इंडिया में जून तो सेप्टेमबर में खरीफ क्रॉप्स ग्रोव होते हैं लाइक दीज। अब अगर मौनसून अच्छा नहीं हुआ तो खरीफ क्रॉप्स के प्रोड़क्शन तो कम होगा ही, इसके साथ साथ रूरल इंकम भी कम होगी, जिसके वज़े से रूरल डिमांड भी कम होगी।
अब ओयल के प्राइसिस के वज़े से इंडिया में अल्रेडी बहुत इंफलेशन प्रेशर है, अब फूड इंफलेशन ना आ जाए, इसके वज़े से गवर्मन्ट ने रिकॉर्ड पल्सिस का बफर बनाया है डबल पर लास्ट यार।
अब गवर्मन्ट ने तो हमें अल्रेडी वॉर्ण कर दिया है कि मौनसून के वज़े से प्रॉब्लेम्स और बढ़ सकती है, बट हीर्स होपिंग के एल नीनो का इंपाक्ट इंडिया पे बहुत ज़्यादा नहीं आता है, लेट सी वोट हैपन्स।
reels.caption:
Finance Minister warned about this challenge for India.

Check out the full reel to know!!

A weak monsoon, rising temperatures, high crude oil prices, and food inflation could put pressure on households, businesses, and overall economic growth in 2026.

A few days ago, IMD confirmed that El Niño has arrived and is expected to strengthen during the monsoon season.

Why does that matter?

Historically, El Niño has been linked to below-normal rainfall and drought-like conditions in India. Since a large part of our agriculture still depends on monsoon rains, lower rainfall could impact kharif crop production.

To prepare for this, the government has built record food grain buffer stocks that can be released if prices rise sharply.

But weaker agricultural output can also affect rural incomes, which may reduce demand and consumption across the economy.

There is some positive news as well.

The US-Iran agreement is expected to be signed on Friday, and if tensions ease further, oil prices could eventually come down. That would provide some relief to India, which imports most of its crude oil.

Hopefully, things improve from here.

Some insights in this reel are inspired by Nithin Kamath’s recent LinkedIn post on how a weak monsoon, rising temperatures, expensive crude oil, and food inflation could affect India’s economy in 2026.

[Anushka Rathod, Finance, India, El Niño, Monsoon]
#anushkarathod #finance #india #elnino #monsoon

reels.transcript: Hi, I'm Seja. I have grown up creating content on the streets of India. When I first started out, I didn't have a fancy studio or a big production setup. I just spent a lot of time observing people in motion. And one place I did that the most was the metro. I think that's where I first became a storyteller. Because I was trying to figure out who my audience was, and the only clues I had were people around me. And somehow, I always started with what they were wearing. Or someone in a simple cotton suit standing in line at Delhi Heart, and imagine what her day was going to be like. Or someone in semi-formals rushing to catch a cab and wonder if they were nervous about their first job. The thing is, I never actually knew their stories. Because clothes aren't just what people wear, they tell us where they're going, what they're dreaming about. That's what fascinates me about India. Every day, millions of people are in motion. Maybe that's why I still love fashion today, and it's not because of the trends. And in a country that's always moving, there are stories everywhere you look. This is my India in Motion. What's yours? Share your story with Canon's India in Motion and enter the contest.
reels.caption:
When I started out on YouTube I spent so much time observing other people- especially on the metro on my way to north campus daily trying to figure out who my audience would be!

reels.transcript: Of the 100 million things that I end up buying on Instagram, this one actually came through for me. So you need to know about it. Story time. 7-8 महीने पहले I saw a reel that अंकुष भहुना had put up on his Instagram about this product. और उस टाइम पे I was struggling so much with finding out what the right retinol or retinal for my skin is. दो-तीन जीज़े ट्राय करी थी, कुछ नहीं चल रहा था. And usually, you know, when you get to the age that I am about to be in, आप जब सबच्छब्बे सटाइस आपके हो जाते हो न, अजीर सा online ही pressure शुरू हो जाता है क्योंकि आपके peers तो आपके जितने ही बेवकुफ होते हैं. Like when I sit down and talk to my friends who are my age and I ask them, listen, what are you doing about retinol? And they have no answers. They're like, what are you doing about retinal? So ये बड़ा एक सरकस चलता रहता है till you actually try a few products and then find out what's actually working for you. For me, that was my journey with Cellomax. जब मैंने अंकुष का वीडियो देखा, I immediately called him. And I think उसके बाद मैं उससे मिली भी थी देली में. जब मैंने उसको बोला, I said, listen, I've ordered this product from your reel. The Cellomax Retinal Shot Tightening Booster. I went from an 8-step routine down to literally a 3-step routine. And I'm going to show you what that is right now. The first thing is to clean any makeup dust pollution off your face. So once my face is nice and clean, I will go in with a toner which is a pore-tight toner. Then we're going to go with the magic product. And it's yellow because it's the pure form of retinol. Also, it's retinal, not retinol. You have to use it like a pore treatment. With this product, less is more. You don't need much, which is why even though this is a 15ml tube, it lasts you really long. So I just do 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7. Like everywhere that I feel like I have hyperpigmentation and pores. And then very little pressure, you just kind of massage it into your skin. And when you do that, you'll feel a prickling sensation because of the micro-needle. I go on top with Cellimax's pore and dark spot cream, which again is like a spot treatment. So I'll do 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. I'll also do it on my lips. I don't even have to do lip balm because this cream works really well as a moisturizer. I just make sure that my face doesn't immediately hit the pillow. So I let it kind of like seep into my skin a little bit. And next morning, I'm glowing. So if you're like me and you're trying to find a solution to pores, to hyperpigmentation, smoothing fine lines, and ऐसा बोला जाता है कि this is like an anti-aging product, that's actually not true. It's worked like magic for me. So I recommend that you try the Cellimax Retinal Tightening Booster Shot. Also, it's from South Korea. Any place that does BBQ so well cannot mess up on skincare. That's my theory.
reels.caption:
God bless the land of barbecue and glass skin!
Celimax’s retinal shot has honestly been a game changer for my skin! If you’ve been wanting to try it, you can find it on @mynykaa and @skinbae.in ✨

reels.transcript: Existential dread. How do you spell dread? Okay, so I had thought that writing a second book would mean sitting in a cafe in Paris with like a coffee and a berry, very aesthetic, very chic, very sane. Here's what nobody tells you. The first one, you're terrified. You write it in silence, you hope for the best, you kind of also dread that nobody's going to end up reading it, and then you pray. But the second one, you know people are waiting, which is so much scarier. So you do the most logical thing there is to do as an adult. You wear the softest, most comfiest pants you own, which are my GoColors cargo sweats, and you curl up on the couch and you type anyway, hoping that your brain's going to come through. Okay, new plan. BRB. Okay, new plan is, when you don't have it together, look like you have it together. Like Queen really said, fake it till you make it. Thank God, I have the right combination. I've got my denim utility pants with eight pockets, so one for my phone, one for snacks, one for ideas, and the rest for existential dread. Now I feel like an author. Let's go. Existential dread. How do you spell dread?

reels.caption:
Welcome to Sleepover With Anushka Sen powered by Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra 

This week, Anushka Sen is joined by Shakti Mohan for a fun and heartwarming girls sleepover filled with dance, music, family stories and honest conversations.

From making hot chocolate together and sharing healthy dessert recipes to talking about relationships, growing up with the Mohan sisters and life beyond the spotlight, Shakti opens up about some of her most cherished memories. She also shares stories from boarding school, being a school topper, her bond with Mukti and Neeti Mohan, and how dance shaped her life.

The girls also play Antakshari with a twist, talk about K-pop and K-dramas, dance together and share their favorite night rituals.

Filmed #withGalaxy S26 Ultra, every sleepover moment feels fun, candid and cinematic. From photos enhanced with the Photo Assist feature to stunning low-light captures using Nightography, every memory is brought to life beautifully 

Watch the most fun, inspiring and honest side of Shakti Mohan only on Hauterrfly.

FilmedwithGalaxyS26Ultra

reels.transcript: Pessimistic और Optimistic Locking दो ऐसे concept हैं जो हर interviewer पूछता है, इसलिए इसको समझते अगले एक मिनिट में. अब Pessimistic Locking में तुम safe play करते हो, यहाँ पे क्या करते हो, तुम्हें कोई भी update वग़ेरा करना होता है, उसके लिए पहले तुम lock ले लेते हो और तब भी कोई update करते हो, ताकि concurrent अगर कोई user आए, तो वो update ना कर पाए same time पे. बट Optimistic Locking में क्या करते हो, तुम version पे खेलते हो, तुम क्या करते हो, एक version define कर देते हो हर update का, और तुम check करते हो कि जब भी update करने जा रहो, वो version match हो रहा है या नहीं हो रहा है. तो मालों user A है, user B है, version 5 current version है. तो user A जब update करने चाहेगा, तो version 6 हो चाहेगा update होके, तो अगर same time पे user B भी try करेगा, तो उस time पे वो update नहीं कर पाएगा कि user ने already version 6 पे update कर दिया है. And that is how it works, तो इसको depth में समझना है, तो comment करते हो, लोग सारी details में DM में मिल जाएंगी, follow code वे तो पासना for more.
reels.caption:
🔒 Pessimistic Locking vs 🚀 Optimistic Locking

When multiple users try to update the same data, how do databases prevent conflicts?

**Pessimistic Locking**
👉 Assumes conflicts are likely.
👉 Locks the record immediately.
👉 Other transactions must wait.
👉 Best for banking, payments, and inventory systems.

Think: “Better safe than sorry.”

**Optimistic Locking**
👉 Assumes conflicts are rare.
👉 No lock during reads.
👉 Uses a version number to detect conflicts during updates.
👉 If data changed, the transaction retries.

Think: “Trust, but verify.”

⚡ Key Difference:

* Pessimistic = Prevent conflicts before they happen.
* Optimistic = Detect conflicts after they happen.

Rule of thumb:
✔ High write contention → Pessimistic Locking
✔ High read, low write workloads → Optimistic Locking

Understanding when to use each can significantly improve system performance, scalability, and data consistency.

#SystemDesign #Database #BackendEngineering #SoftwareEngineering #Java SpringBoot DistributedSystems TechLearning Architecture Concurrency

reels.transcript: Shopify monolith architecture follow karke 30 TB of data per minute handle kar paata hai. How they are able to do so? Isko depth mein samajhte hain. Toh hota kya hai ye modular monolith architecture follow karte hain, jisme ek bada sa codebase hota hai, jisme different components likhe hota hain. Ab ye ek component doosre se overlap na kare, iske liye inke paas tools wakara hai to detect that part as well. Iske baad ye hexagonal architecture utilization karke interaction ko handle karte hain. Mal lo koi API request aayi toh wo pehle API adapter pe jaayegi, uske baad application ko logic pe jaayegi and then database layer pe jaayegi. So agar kabhi tumhe do parts ko change bhi karna ho tab bhi tumhara business logic safe rahega usme changes ki zarurat nahi hai. Now sabse badi baat aati hai deployment ki, ki itna bada codebase kaise koi pod handle kar paayega. Toh inhone yaha pe kya kar rakha hai ki har pod jo hai ek mini shopify samajh lo usme kuch stores deployed hai. Yaani ki mal lo Kylie Jenner ka ek pod pe deployed hai aur wo crash ho gaya toh baaki saare stores safe rahenge and that is how they are able to handle everything on a monolith architecture. reels.caption:
Many developers think Terraform and Ansible do the same thing.

They don’t. 👇

🏗️ Terraform creates infrastructure.
Think: VPCs, EC2s, Load Balancers, RDS, Kubernetes Clusters.

⚙️ Ansible configures infrastructure.
Think: Install Docker, Java, Nginx, deploy applications, manage server settings.

A simple way to remember:

Terraform: “Give me 10 servers.”

Ansible: “Now configure those 10 servers.”

In real-world DevOps pipelines, they work together:

Code → Terraform → Infrastructure → Ansible → Application Deployment 🚀

Understanding this difference is one of the fundamentals every DevOps and Cloud Engineer should know.

#Terraform #Ansible #DevOps #CloudComputing #AWS Kubernetes InfrastructureAsCode PlatformEngineering SoftwareEngineering DevOpsEngineer CloudEngineer TechExplained

reels.caption:
Ever wondered how Elasticsearch finds results in milliseconds across millions of records? 🤔

The secret is not faster hardware.
It’s a smarter data structure.

Instead of scanning every document, Elasticsearch creates an **Inverted Index**:

“docker” → Doc1, Doc5, Doc20
“redis” → Doc2, Doc8, Doc20

When you search “redis”, it directly jumps to matching documents instead of searching everything.

⚡ Data is split into Shards for scalability
⚡ Replicas provide fault tolerance
⚡ Searches run in parallel across nodes
⚡ Results are ranked and merged before being returned

That’s why product searches, log analytics, and observability platforms can search billions of records in milliseconds.

Elasticsearch isn’t just a database.
It’s a distributed search engine built for speed.

reels.transcript: कोई फ़रक नहीं पड़ता तुम student हो, software engineer हो, DevOps engineer हो, CI, CD कैसा concept है जो हर किसी को पता होना चाहिए and इसको cover करेंगे अगले 40 second में. Now, CI stands for continuous integration. किसी भी company में एक साथ बहुत सारा development हो रहा होता है और बहुत सारा code GitHub पे push हो रहा होता है. इसके लिए बहुत सरूरी है कि एक automatic process हो जो तुम्हारा build बना दे, test cases रन कर दे and errors check कर ले. और इसी process को करने के लिए हम एक CI pipeline बना दे जो ये काम automatically करता है. इसी को continuous integration कहते हैं. On the other hand, CD stands for continuous delivery and continuous deployment. Continuous delivery का मतलब होता है कि एक automatic process जो तुम्हारा जैसे ही तुम्हारा code ready है to deploy उसका एक package वगेरा बना दे. यहां पर तुम्हें approval की ज़रूप पड़ते है before deployment. बट अगर हम approval को हटा दे and automatically package बना के deploy भी हो जाया तो उसको कहते है continuous deployment. इसको depth में पढ़ना बहुत ज़रूरी है इसलिए comment कर दो CI इन सारी details तुम्हें comment या DM में मिल जाएंगी.
